headline: KSE-100 down a 3rd straight session (171,739 Thu, −0.7% more Fri intraday to ~170,475) on US-Iran-Israel escalation and oil spike — now ~9.4-10.6% below the Jan 23 all-time high (189,556), i.e. at/just past the 10% correction line; begin deploying dry powder into MZNPETF/MIIETF; portfolio ~+1.6%, no holding moved >5%; AVN still sliding in the TRIM zone; EFERT H1 results Wed Jul 29 (5 days out)
market: The KSE-100 closed Thursday Jul 23 at 171,739.45, down 2,690.48 points (−1.54%) — a third straight losing session — on intensifying US-Iran-Israel conflict and firmer oil prices. Friday Jul 24 opened weaker and was down 1,264.14 points (−0.74%) to 170,475.30 (intraday low 169,512.88) when trading was suspended for Friday prayers; the session had not yet resumed/closed at review time, so today's number is provisional. Measured against the all-time high of 189,556 set Jan 23, 2026, the index is now roughly 9.4% below peak at Thursday's close and 10.1–10.6% below peak using Friday's intraday levels — this crosses the 10% drawdown line that flips the correction/dry-powder switch for the first time since the Jan rally. Per the standing plan, begin deploying part of the ~Rs 400K dry-powder cash funds (AICF/MICF) into the equity index ETFs now, keeping a reserve in case the drawdown extends toward 15%. SBP's first FY27 MPC meeting is now just 3 days out (Jul 27, 2026); 97% of surveyed analysts still expect the policy rate held at 11.5% given the geopolitical backdrop and firmer oil. No fresh IMF news. Your portfolio, marked to Thursday's close (Friday's PSX prices had not yet posted at review time): ~Rs 9.65M value on ~Rs 9.50M invested (+1.6% unrealized, down from +2.7% Wednesday/Thursday) — broad, shallow declines across nearly every line (roughly −0.2% to −3% each), consistent with the index move; no holding moved more than 3% day-over-day. AVN slipped again to Rs 32.06 (from Rs 32.67) with no fresh company-specific news — still no PSX filing addressing the FY25 earnings collapse behind the TRIM call — and remains squarely in/below the Rs 32–33 sell zone: this is now seven straight reviews flagging it with the price continuing to grind lower (Rs 34.24 → 33.11 → 32.82 → 32.67 → 32.06) and no bounce materializing. EFERT's board still meets Wed Jul 29, 2026, 9am for H1 results/dividend — now 5 days away, outside the 3-day notification window but close; will alert when it crosses inside 3 days. CPHL's REIT demerger remains exactly where it was Jul 8 (board-approved, awaiting shareholder/court/regulatory sign-off) — checked again, no movement. No ex-dividend dates found within the next 3 days for any holding.

actions.title: Avanceon — fundamentals broken, price still sliding, not bouncing
actions.detail: Your largest stock position (Rs 800K in) and worst performer (−28.9%, now at Rs 32.06 vs Rs 32.67 last review). FY25 net income fell ~68%, Q3 revenue fell 44%, margins collapsed from 12% to 4%, 3-year shareholder return −32%. Seven straight reviews have now flagged this and the stock keeps moving the wrong way — Rs 34.24 → 33.11 → 32.82 → 32.67 → 32.06 — with no company-specific news to explain a bounce. Don't keep waiting for a rebound that isn't coming — sell half at market now, around Rs 32-33 (it's already trading at the low end of that zone). No catalyst expected before the Sep 2, 2026 earnings report; exit the rest fully if that report shows no recovery.
actions.target: Sell ~12,000 shares now near Rs 32-33, split proceeds ~60% into MEBL / ~40% into MIIETF

actions.title: Meezan Bank — core compounder
actions.detail: +45% and Pakistan's premier Islamic bank with structural deposit advantage. This is exactly the 20-year stock you want. Tuesday's selloff pulled it from Rs 547 to Rs 529 — still above the Rs 500 add line, but getting closer. Add on any 10%+ dip.

actions.title: Faysal Bank — Islamic conversion paying off
actions.detail: +44% since purchase. Strong deposit growth post Islamic conversion, good dividends. Long-term keep.

actions.title: Fatima Fertilizer
actions.detail: +41% with concessionary-gas cost advantage and solid dividends. Keep; it funds itself.

actions.title: Engro Fertilizers — dividend cut, inventory glut, H1 results dated
actions.detail: −5.8% on cost (Rs 187.49 today), ~7% dividend yield. Q1 2026 urea offtake fell 73% QoQ and EFERT holds 48% of industry inventory; payout was trimmed to 80%. Discounts fully withdrawn Apr 4, 2026 (market share down to ~27%); new CEO Imran Ahmed joined May 11 — no verdict yet on his tenure. A Jul 17 PSX filing confirms the board meets Wednesday, Jul 29, 2026 at 9am to approve H1 results and consider a dividend — now 5 days out. Keep for yield but if the Jul 29 print shows continued weak offtake, rotate into MEBL or an ETF.
actions.target: Re-evaluate at the Jul 29, 2026 H1 results; if weak, sell and split proceeds ~60% MEBL / ~40% MZNPETF

actions.title: Mughal Iron & Steel — cyclical winner
actions.detail: +24%. Steel is cyclical; enjoy the construction upcycle but be ready to trim if it doubles from cost. Not a forever stock.

actions.title: Citi Pharma — fine, just re-rating, plus a REIT demerger in motion
actions.detail: −11% (mostly broad-market weakness — Tuesday's geopolitical selloff plus the earlier Jul 8 dip) but fundamentals are intact: FY25 revenue +6%, earnings +7%, ~4% dividend yield, low volatility (beta 0.51). Board approved a REIT demerger on Jul 8 — two Lahore properties (4.1 and 27.15 kanal) move into a new SPV, Citi Core Holdings, in exchange for 331.72M new Citi Core shares issued back to CPHL. Potential long-term value-unlock from carving out real estate; no update since the Jul 8 announcement. No reason to sell; also no urgency to add at ~20x forward earnings.

actions.title: Meezan Pakistan ETF — your best vehicle for new money
actions.detail: −12% is entry-timing plus Tuesday's broad selloff, nothing more. For a 10–25 year horizon, broad Shariah-compliant index exposure is where fresh contributions and dividends should go. Averages out single-stock risk like AVN.

actions.title: Mahaana Islamic Index ETF
actions.detail: Same logic as MZNPETF — low-cost index compounding. Add regularly.

actions.title: Air Link — high beta, policy-dependent
actions.detail: −3% after Tuesday's selloff. Mobile-assembly economics depend on import/tax policy. Fine to hold, but cap position size and trim on sharp rallies.

actions.title: Fauji Foods — turnaround, keep small
actions.detail: +8% and newly profitable, but still a turnaround story. Keep the position small; take profits if it runs 50%+.

actions.title: Bestway Cement
actions.detail: +6%. Cement upcycle with infrastructure spending; dividend payer. Hold.

actions.title: National Foods
actions.detail: +5%. Consumer staple compounder — hold for decades.

actions.title: GlaxoSmithKline Pakistan
actions.detail: Roughly flat. Defensive pharma with drug-pricing deregulation tailwind. Hold.

actions.title: Searle
actions.detail: +9%. Pharma margin recovery underway post-deregulation. Hold.

actions.title: Ghani Glass
actions.detail: +12%. Solid industrial. Hold.

actions.title: Dolmen City REIT
actions.detail: +22% plus steady rental dividends. Good inflation hedge; hold.

actions.title: Unilever Pakistan Foods
actions.detail: +21% but only 3 shares (~Rs 76K). Exceptional company, illiquid stock. Hold; immaterial to portfolio decisions.

actions.title: Al-Ameen Shariah Stock Fund
actions.detail: +7% — your largest fund. Actively managed Islamic equity; solid long-term vehicle. Hold and let it compound.

actions.title: Meezan Islamic Fund
actions.detail: +12%. Flagship Islamic equity fund. Hold.

actions.title: NBP Islamic Stock Fund
actions.detail: +20%. Hold.

actions.title: AKD Islamic Stock Fund
actions.detail: Roughly flat after Tuesday's dip. Hold.

actions.title: NBP Islamic Sarmaya Izafa — balanced fund
actions.detail: +2%. Balanced allocation gives smoother rides; fine to keep as ballast.

actions.title: Al-Ameen Islamic Cash Fund — start deploying, correction line crossed
actions.detail: Rs ~201K parked at money-market rates. The KSE-100 is now ~9.4-10.6% below its Jan 23, 2026 all-time high (189,556) on the US-Iran-Israel escalation — right at/just past the 10% correction threshold that was the trigger to start deploying this reserve. Don't dump it all at once (the drawdown could still extend toward 15%); redeem about half now.
actions.target: Redeem ~Rs 100K now, split ~60% into MZNPETF / ~40% into MIIETF; keep the remaining ~Rs 100K in reserve in case the market falls further toward a 15% drawdown

actions.title: Mahaana Islamic Cash Fund — start deploying, correction line crossed
actions.detail: Same situation as AICF — Rs ~201K reserve, correction threshold now crossed. Redeem about half now, keep the rest as reserve.
actions.target: Redeem ~Rs 100K now, split ~60% into MZNPETF / ~40% into MIIETF; keep the remaining ~Rs 100K in reserve in case the market falls further toward a 15% drawdown

actions.title: Dust position (~Rs 2,300)
actions.detail: Too small to matter. Redeem into a main fund next time you transact, just to declutter.

actions.title: Dust position (~Rs 235)
actions.detail: Same — consolidate away.

allocation.rationale: The KSE-100 has now crossed the 10% drawdown line from its Jan 23, 2026 all-time high (189,556) amid the US-Iran-Israel escalation. Per the standing plan, new money shifts heavily toward the broad Islamic index ETFs while shares are on sale; the cash-fund slice is trimmed since that reserve is now the thing being deployed, not built up further. A Meezan Bank slice stays available if it dips below Rs 500, and the actively managed fund leg is trimmed slightly to fund the extra ETF weight.

items.name: Meezan Pakistan ETF
items.note: Broad index exposure — the default home for new money, extra weight while the market is down ~10% from peak

items.name: Mahaana Islamic Index ETF
items.note: Second index leg, low cost — also getting extra weight during the correction

items.name: Meezan Bank
items.note: Only below Rs 500 — otherwise this slice goes to the others

items.name: Al-Ameen Islamic Cash Fund
items.note: Dry powder — now being deployed rather than built up further; see AICF/MICF actions above

notes: Big picture for a 10–25 year horizon: (1) Your only real problem is AVN — a large position with deteriorating fundamentals that's now six reviews overdue for actually selling, and the price keeps sliding while you wait; fix that first. (2) The KSE-100 just crossed the 10% drawdown line from its Jan 23, 2026 peak (189,556) on the Middle East escalation — this is the correction the ~Rs 400K in AICF/MICF was held for; start deploying about half of it into MZNPETF/MIIETF now per the actions above, keep the rest in reserve in case it extends toward 15%. (3) Direct all other new savings into the ETFs and equity funds rather than adding more single-stock names — concentration check: 18 stocks + 9 funds = 27 positions is still fine as-is apart from resolving AVN; the two fertiliser names (EFERT/FATIMA) and pharma names (GLAXO/SEARL/CPHL) have some thematic overlap but each has a distinct enough thesis to keep separately for now. (4) Reinvest all dividends. (5) Expect 10–15% drawdowns like this one; they are the price of the 40%+ years — don't panic-sell the core holdings, just deploy the dry powder as planned. (6) Dust positions UPFL (~Rs 76K, illiquid but fine to hold) and AIAIP/AKDISIF (fund dust, ~Rs 2,500 combined) remain flagged for consolidation whenever you next transact — not urgent. (7) Mark Jul 29, 2026 as the date to actually check EFERT's H1 results — it's a confirmed board-meeting date, now 5 days out.
disclaimer: Automated research by your Claude portfolio agent (last reviewed 24 Jul 2026) — a decision aid, not licensed financial advice. Prices/NAVs may lag. Verify with your broker before trading.
